title: if and else - choosing a path
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: if runs its block when the condition is true; else covers everything else.
explanation: A condition in C is just a number: zero is false, anything else is true. The modulo operator % gives the remainder, the usual way to test even or odd.

exercises.question: What does this print?
hints: The condition is false, so else runs.
0 == 0 is true, so the if block runs.

exercises.question: In C, which value counts as false?
hints: C has no separate bool in the old style - it uses numbers.
